flag: Slightly awkward coordination: 'tai maanantaisin tammikuussa' — the month qualifier placement after the weekday is grammatically acceptable but creates a structural imbalance; natural Finnish might prefer 'tai tammikuun maanantaisin'

'5 minuutin kohdalla klo 9.05–17.05' is structurally confusing: '5 minuutin kohdalla' (at the 5-minute mark) followed by a range that already encodes minutes; the phrase does not naturally convey the intended meaning and reads as a literal mistranslation of a machine-parsed expression

'tammikuun ja heinäkuun 1. päivänä' — genitive coordination with a single ordinal is grammatically borderline; more natural: 'tammikuun 1. ja heinäkuun 1. päivänä'; also 'tai maanantaisin tammikuussa ja heinäkuussa' — acceptable but wordy
'sunnuntaisin ja lauantaisin' — conventional order in Finnish is 'lauantaisin ja sunnuntaisin' (Saturday before Sunday); minor register issue

Very long, complex sentence with multiple stacked qualifiers that strains Finnish sentence structure; 'kesäkuun 1.–5. päivänä tai maanantaista perjantaihin kesäkuussa' — redundant double month reference (kesäkuun…kesäkuussa) within same clause is awkward; overall comprehensibility is poor
'joulukuun 1.–5. päivänä tai sunnuntaisin joulukuussa' — double month reference (joulukuun…joulukuussa) within the same clause is slightly redundant and awkward, though grammatically tolerable

'0–30 minuutin kohdalla, klo 9, 11, 13, 15 ja 17' — comma after the minute phrase before 'klo' is non-standard; 'klo 9, 11, 13, 15 ja 17' lists clock hours without minutes, inconsistent with the minute-range prefix; the phrase reads as a fragment and is structurally confusing
'joka kolmannen kuukauden 1.–5. päivänä' is grammatically correct but 'joka kolmas kuukausi' construction with genitive here is somewhat formal/stiff; acceptable in written register

'klo 9–20 ja 22' — listing a range and then an isolated hour with 'ja' is grammatically odd; normally the isolated hour would be listed first or the range reformulated; comma before 'klo' is missing for parallel structure consistency with similar sentences in the set

'5 ja 30 sekunnin kohdalla' mixed with '15 minuutin välein' in the same phrase creates a confusing double-cadence structure; comma after 'kohdalla' before '15 minuutin välein' is present but the coordination of two different time-granularity expressions (seconds + minutes) without a clear connector reads as ill-formed Finnish
